claims.claim: AutoMem treats memory management as a trainable metamemory skill with dual optimization loops
claims.evidence: Stanford AutoMem paper abstract: structure-revision loop + proficiency-training loop

claims.claim: Memory-only optimization yields ~2-4x gains on Crafter/MiniHack/NetHack without changing task-action weights
claims.evidence: Table 1 in AutoMem paper; HuggingFace paper page

claims.claim: Qwen2.5-32B with AutoMem competitive with Claude Opus 4.5 on long-horizon game benchmarks
claims.evidence: Paper results section; cross-check benchmark numbers in Table 1

claims.claim: Memory specialist co-processor with LoRA adapter required in agent-rules
claims.evidence: Brain repo is procedural harness; no weight fine-tuning. Adapt pattern only.

claims.claim: File-system memory ops (log/plan/read/write) as first-class actions separate from task skills
claims.evidence: Maps to memory_hierarchy/, context_sync, MCP file tools — implement as skills not LoRA

claims.claim: DeepSeek GRPO/MLA architecture changes for agent-rules
claims.evidence: 47 sources mostly DeepSeek background; not AutoMem core. Background only.
